Seqqual reported separately includes fair compatibility track optimized compact track.

title: Benchmarking BamScale Across Step1, GAlignments, and SeqQual Workloads
text: benchmark suite covers three distinct access patterns: step1: alignment-metadata extraction without sequence/quality materialization. good proxy BAM filtering, fragment-length profiling, fragment-distribution QC. galignments: construction alignment objects suitable Bioconductor workflows centered GenomicAlignments. fair compatibility mode, closest comparator Rsamtools::scanBam optimized compact mode, reduces internal overhead interpreted optimized BamScale track rather strict apples--apples replacement compatibility path Across runs, benchmark design emphasizes: deterministic case order balanced BamScale worker/thread plans explicit comparator baselines single-file multi-file reporting fixed 48-thread budget multi-file plans

title: Benchmarking BamScale Across Step1, GAlignments, and SeqQual Workloads
text: underlying four selected BAMs used runs, repeated files allowed populate 12-file multi-file benchmark set.

title: Benchmarking BamScale Across Step1, GAlignments, and SeqQual Workloads
text: combined benchmark supports following conclusions: BamScale showed clear single-file best-case multi-file advantages step1 galignments, strongest multi-file points occurring low-worker, high-thread regime. seqqual, BamScale’s fair compatibility track remained slower Rsamtools comparator, optimized compact track substantially reduced gap produced clear single-file win. multi-file seqqual, compact track improved fair compatibility path surpass best Rsamtools multi-file baseline run. Overall, step1 galignments clearest BamScale strengths, seqqual compact mode represents substantial optimization improves, fully close, multi-file gap comparator.

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: BamScale multithreaded BAM processing package R built top ompBAM C++ engine. designed Bioconductor users need high-throughput BAM parsing preserving familiar Rsamtools GenomicAlignments workflow patterns.

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: BamScale focuses three goals: speed modern multi-core systems, compatibility common Bioconductor input/output contracts, transparent benchmarking reproducibility. many Bioconductor pipelines, BAM access still major bottleneck. core bottleneck BAM decompression , also fact existing R-facing workflows often rely effectively single-threaded per-file access patterns. BamScale addresses exposing OpenMP threading ompBAM engine directly R interface, still returning Bioconductor-friendly objects. practical design goal replace familiar Bioconductor workflows separate ecosystem. Instead, BamScale aims preserve way users already work : Rsamtools::scanBam()-style field extraction, GenomicAlignments::readGAlignments()-style alignment-object workflows, BiocParallel file-level execution multiple BAMs processed together. means BamScale intended fit existing tooling already fits, additional within-file threading axis can remove current parsing bottleneck alignment-centric workloads. Key capabilities: OpenMP-enabled per-file parallelism via threads, optional multi-file parallelism via BiocParallel (BPPARAM), ScanBamParam-like filtering (mapqFilter, flag, , , tag), data.frame S4Vectors::DataFrame, GenomicAlignments::GAlignments, GenomicAlignments::GAlignmentPairs, scanBam-shaped list output (= "scanBam"). , step1 refers common alignment-metadata extraction workload built fields : qname flag rname pos mapq cigar kind BAM access pattern used many downstream QC, filtering, fragment-level summary workflows.

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: BamScale designed familiar users already work Rsamtools GenomicAlignments. step1-style metadata extraction maps naturally onto scanBam()-like use cases. = "GAlignments" = "GAlignmentPairs" support alignment-object workflows used downstream Bioconductor. BiocParallel integration preserves standard file-level parallel workflow model. main added capability BamScale can also use multiple threads within BAM file ompBAM. key difference baseline workflow pattern main reason can remove read-parsing bottlenecks workloads metadata extraction alignment-object construction.

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: R C++17 toolchain OpenMP-capable compiler/runtime ompBAM available R library

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("BiocManager") } BiocManager::install("ompBAM")  if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_github("cparsania/BamScale")

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
code: library(BamScale)  bam <- ompBAM::example_BAM("Unsorted")  # 1) Step1-style extraction x <- bam_read(   file = bam,   what = c("qname", "flag", "rname", "pos", "mapq", "cigar"),   threads = 4 )  # 2) Seq/qual in comparator-compatible mode sq <- bam_read(   file = bam,   what = c("qname", "seq", "qual"),   as = "data.frame",   seqqual_mode = "compatible",   threads = 4 )  # 3) Fast chromosome-level counts cnt <- bam_count(file = bam, threads = 4)

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: BamScale can parallelize two axes: across files via BPPARAM workers, within file via OpenMP threads. Approximate effective concurrency: min(length(file), bpnworkers(BPPARAM)) * threads auto_threads = TRUE, BamScale preserves higher per-file thread counts possible reducing number concurrently active file workers shrinking per-file threads.

code: bam_count(   file,   param = NULL,   threads = 1L,   BPPARAM = NULL,   auto_threads = FALSE,   include_unmapped = TRUE )

title: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count
text: file BAM input (character, BamFile, BamFileList). param Optional Rsamtools::ScanBamParam (compatible list). threads Requested number OpenMP threads. May capped auto_threads = TRUE. BPPARAM Optional BiocParallel parameter multi-file operation. auto_threads Logical; TRUE BPPARAM multiple workers, BamScale adaptively avoids oversubscription preserving higher per-file OpenMP thread counts possible reducing number concurrently active file workers shrinking per-file threads. include_unmapped Whether include extra * row unmapped records.

title: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count
text: one file: data.frame columns seqname, seqlength, count. multiple files: named list data.frames.

title: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count
text: Parallelism behavior matches bam_read(): BPPARAM distributes work across BAM files, threads controls OpenMP work within file. auto_threads = TRUE BPPARAM multiple workers, BamScale first limits number concurrently active workers preserve requested per-file thread count within detected core budget, caps per-file OpenMP threads single file still oversubscribe machine.

what: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
text: bam_read() multithreaded sequential BAM reader built top ompBAM. interface designed familiar users Rsamtools::scanBam(), GenomicAlignments::readGAlignments(), GenomicAlignments::readGAlignmentPairs().

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
code: bam_read(   file,   param = NULL,   what = NULL,   tag = NULL,   as = c("DataFrame", "data.frame", "GAlignments", "GAlignmentPairs", "scanBam"),   seqqual_mode = c("compatible", "compact"),   threads = 1L,   BPPARAM = NULL,   auto_threads = FALSE,   use.names = FALSE,   with.which_label = FALSE,   include_unmapped = TRUE )

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
text: file BAM input. Supported values : single BAM path (character(1)) multiple BAM paths, Rsamtools::BamFile, Rsamtools::BamFileList. param Optional Rsamtools::ScanBamParam (compatible list lightweight use). following fields honored: mapqFilter, flag, , , tag. Character vector fields return, similar scanBam(=...). Supported fields qname, flag, rname, strand, pos, qwidth, mapq, cigar, mrnm, mpos, isize, seq, qual. tag Character vector 2-letter tag names extract. Output format: "DataFrame": returns S4Vectors::DataFrame (default), "data.frame": returns base data.frame, "GAlignments": returns GenomicAlignments::GAlignments, "GAlignmentPairs": returns GenomicAlignments::GAlignmentPairs, "scanBam": returns scanBam()-shaped list--lists. seqqual_mode Controls representation seq/qual fields requested: "compatible" (default): return character vectors matching scanBam-style expectations, "compact": return raw list-columns faster/lower-overhead extraction. mode currently supported = "data.frame" = "DataFrame". threads Requested number OpenMP threads used reading/decompression. May capped auto_threads = TRUE. BPPARAM Optional BiocParallel parameter used file contains one BAM. NULL, files processed serially. auto_threads Logical; TRUE BPPARAM multiple workers, BamScale adaptively avoids oversubscription preserving higher per-file OpenMP thread counts possible reducing number concurrently active file workers shrinking per-file threads. use.names Passed alignment object conversion. TRUE, read names (qname) used object names. .which_label Logical; TRUE param includes , extra which_label column returned. include_unmapped Logical; whether unmapped records retained (subject param$flag constraints).

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
text: file length 1: one object format specified . file length > 1 (BamFileList): named list outputs, one per BAM file.

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
text: bam_read() intentionally column-compatible common BAM fields used Bioconductor workflows can used fast drop-reader conversion downstream classes. Parallelism model: BPPARAM parallelizes across files (one file per BiocParallel worker). threads parallelizes within file via OpenMP. Effective total concurrency approximately min(length(file), BiocParallel::bpnworkers(BPPARAM)) * threads. auto_threads = TRUE BPPARAM multiple workers, BamScale first limits number concurrently active workers preserve requested per-file thread count within detected core budget, caps per-file OpenMP threads single file still oversubscribe machine. Compatibility notes: Region filtering via param$supported sequential filter (index-jump random access). Flag filtering uses ScanBamFlag semantics converting logical flag requirements required-set required-unset bit masks. Tag values returned character columns. Scalar tags scalar strings; B tags comma-separated vectors. seqqual_mode = "compact" optimized throughput-oriented benchmarking returns raw list-columns seq/qual. "GAlignments" "GAlignmentPairs" output exclude unmapped records. = "scanBam" returns strict scan-like list--lists: without param$, returns one unnamed batch; param$, returns one batch per range label (including empty ranges), requested fields tag values $tag. Biostrings installed, seq qual returned DNAStringSet PhredQuality closer scanBam() compatibility.

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
code: if (requireNamespace("ompBAM", quietly = TRUE)) {   bam <- ompBAM::example_BAM("Unsorted")    # Familiar scanBam-like field selection   x <- bam_read(bam, what = c("qname", "flag", "rname", "pos", "cigar"))    # Include sequence + quality   y <- bam_read(bam, what = c("qname", "seq", "qual"), threads = 2)    # scanBam-shaped output   z <- bam_read(bam, what = c("qname", "flag"), tag = "NM", as = "scanBam") }
